cross_references.rule: output.name must be a recognizable form of the company name

grounding_checks.description: If narrative asserts growth, actual revenue CAGR must not be negative
If narrative asserts decline, actual revenue CAGR must not be strongly positive
Revenue drivers should not be listed if agent-bundle has no revenue data at all
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: output.revenue_drivers.length > 0 AND no revenue entries in timeseries

grounding_checks.description: Flag numeric claims in narrative that cannot be traced to agent-bundle or Item 1 text

evidence_quality.prompt: Are revenue drivers and business claims backed by specific figures, segment names, or filing references — not vague assertions like 'the company sells products'?
evidence_quality.score_5: Every driver tied to a specific segment, figure, or filing fact
evidence_quality.score_1: Mostly generic, reads like a Wikipedia stub

completeness.prompt: Does the output cover all major business lines, customer segments, geographies, pricing, and recurrence? Are obvious things missing for this company/industry?
completeness.score_5: All products, segments, geos, pricing, recurrence covered — nothing obvious missing
completeness.score_1: Major business lines or segments omitted

consistency.prompt: Do pricing_model, recurrence_mechanisms, revenue_drivers, and narrative all tell a coherent story? Any contradictions between structured fields and the narrative?
consistency.score_5: All fields align — narrative explains what structured fields encode
consistency.score_3: Minor misalignment (e.g., narrative mentions subscription but pricing_model says one_time)

red_flags.prompt: Is the output free of marketing language, hallucinated products/segments, and vague hand-waving? Does it read like analyst research, not a press release?
red_flags.score_3: 1 minor issue (slightly promotional phrase or unverifiable detail)
red_flags.score_1: Marketing copy, made-up specifics, or empty filler

notes: Sprint 1 is extraction-heavy. Grounding surface is limited since business_profile is mostly qualitative. Later sprints (moat, risks, thesis) will have denser grounding checks against quantitative data. The 12/20 LLM threshold is provisional — will be calibrated during Phase 1A-review with human scoring.
